rationale: Product assembly is primarily manual work requiring physical dexterity to fit components together. While industrial robotics can automate some assembly lines, many assembly tasks still need human adaptability for varied products.

title: Engineering Production Workers
rationale: Engineering production work involves operating machinery, handling materials, and performing physical manufacturing tasks. AI may optimise production scheduling, but the hands-on operation of equipment remains human-dependent.

title: Veterinary Nurses
rationale: Veterinary nursing requires physical handling of animals, assisting in surgeries, administering treatments, and monitoring patients. AI can help with diagnostic support and record-keeping, but the core caregiving work demands physical presence and animal handling skills.

title: Other Farm, Forestry and Garden Workers
rationale: These roles are fundamentally physical outdoor work — farming, forestry operations, and garden maintenance. AI has minimal practical application to these hands-on agricultural and horticultural tasks.

title: Veterinarians
rationale: Veterinarians combine knowledge work (diagnosis, treatment planning) with physical examination and surgery. AI can assist with diagnostic imaging and treatment recommendations, but physical examination and procedures require hands-on expertise.

title: Service Station Attendants
rationale: Service station work involves physical tasks like fuel dispensing, basic vehicle checks, and retail sales. Automation through self-service pumps reduces some roles, but the remaining attendant work is primarily physical and interpersonal.

title: Panelbeaters
rationale: Panel beating is skilled manual trades work — reshaping, repairing, and replacing damaged vehicle body panels. It requires physical skill, spatial judgment, and hands-on craftsmanship that AI cannot replicate.

title: Other Accommodation and Hospitality Managers
rationale: Hospitality management blends operational oversight with administrative and customer service tasks. AI can streamline bookings, revenue management, and reporting, but managing staff and guest experiences requires human presence and interpersonal skills.

title: Printers
rationale: Printing trades involve operating and maintaining printing machinery, colour matching, and quality control. While pre-press design work is increasingly digital, the physical operation of printing equipment and hands-on quality assurance remain manual.

title: Vehicle Painters
rationale: Vehicle painting is skilled manual work — surface preparation, colour mixing, spray application, and finishing. It requires physical dexterity and an eye for colour matching that AI cannot perform.

title: Clothing Trades Workers
rationale: Clothing trades involve cutting, sewing, and finishing garments — primarily manual work requiring physical dexterity. AI can assist with pattern design, but the core fabrication work remains hands-on.

title: Visual Merchandisers
rationale: Visual merchandising combines creative design skills with physical store setup. AI can generate display concepts, analyse sales data for layout optimisation, and create digital mockups, but physical installation and spatial judgment in-store still require human presence.

title: Vehicle Body Builders and Trimmers
rationale: Building and trimming vehicle bodies is skilled manual trades work — fabricating, fitting, and finishing vehicle interiors and exteriors. Core tasks require physical craftsmanship with minimal digital component.

title: Safety Inspectors
rationale: Safety inspection combines on-site physical inspection with report writing, compliance analysis, and regulatory knowledge. AI can assist with documentation, checklist analysis, and regulatory updates, but physical site visits and judgment calls require human presence.

title: Other Clerical and Office Support Workers
rationale: Clerical and office support work is predominantly digital — filing, data management, correspondence, and administrative tasks performed on computers. AI can automate much of this routine information processing work.

title: Street Vendors and Related Salespersons
rationale: Street vending is physical, in-person sales work — preparing, displaying, and selling goods in public spaces. It requires physical presence and direct human interaction with minimal digital component.

title: Teachers of English to Speakers of Other Languages
rationale: TESOL teaching involves significant knowledge work — lesson planning, assessment, and language instruction. AI language tutors and translation tools are advancing rapidly, potentially reshaping how language is taught, though classroom management and cultural sensitivity still benefit from human teachers.

title: Mixed Crop and Livestock Farm Workers
rationale: Mixed farming is physically demanding outdoor work — planting, harvesting, animal husbandry, and property maintenance. AI has minimal practical application to these hands-on agricultural tasks.

title: Nurserypersons
rationale: Nursery work involves growing, propagating, and maintaining plants — physical horticultural work in outdoor or greenhouse settings. AI has minimal application to the hands-on plant care tasks.

title: Upholsterers
rationale: Upholstery is skilled manual trades work — cutting, sewing, and fitting fabric and padding to furniture. It requires physical craftsmanship and material handling that AI cannot perform.

title: Textile and Footwear Production Machine Operators
rationale: Operating textile and footwear machinery is primarily physical work — feeding materials, monitoring machines, and handling output. Industrial automation may reduce some roles, but machine operation and troubleshooting remain manual.

title: Graphic Pre-press Trades Workers
rationale: Pre-press work is almost entirely computer-based — preparing digital files, colour correction, layout proofing, and print-ready output. AI design tools and automated preflighting are rapidly advancing in this digital-first domain.
